description: Removing a leaked secret in a new commit without rotating leaves a live credential that remains valid and retrievable from history.
body: Failure mode: a discovered secret is deleted in the latest commit and considered fixed, but the value is unchanged and still present in prior commits. Detection signals: a history scan still flags the value; the credential continues to authenticate; only HEAD was cleaned. Do not treat deletion as remediation. Safer fallback: rotate and revoke the exposed credential first, then scrub it from full history with coordination, rescan to confirm, and record the incident, since exposure cannot be undone by deletion alone.
